accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context: EO 14323 of July 30, 2025 imposed a 40% duty on Brazilian articles and section 202 of the NEA (50 U.S.C. 1622) allows Congress to terminate emergencies via joint resolution.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the description of the NEA mechanism and EO 14323's purpose is accurate context, not invention.

states_purpose.why: The summary clearly states the bill terminates the national emergency declared in EO 14323 imposing duties on Brazilian imports.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly notes the resolution invokes section 202 of the National Emergencies Act and does not amend other statutes.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no exceptions or conditions, which the summary correctly notes.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective date or sunset; the summary accurately states this and notes effect upon enactment.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the sole operative provision—termination of the EO 14323 emergency under the NEA.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

coherent.why: The summary is well-organized and internally consistent.

concise.why: The summary is brief and free of padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: EO 14323, the National Emergencies Act section 202 (50 U.S.C. 1622), and the Fed. Reg. citation are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The date July 30, 2025 and Fed. Reg. citation 90 Fed. Reg. 37739 match the bill text and verified sources.
